sn9.11:1.1: I have heard that on one occasion a certain monk was staying among the Kosalans in a forest grove.
sn9.11:1.2: Now at that time, he spent the day’s abiding thinking evil, unskillful thoughts:
sn9.11:1.3: i.e., thoughts of sensuality, thoughts of ill will, thoughts of doing harm.
sn9.11:1.4: Then the devatā inhabiting the forest grove, feeling sympathy for the monk, desiring his benefit, desiring to bring him to his senses, approached him and addressed him with this verse:
sn9.11:2.2: you’re being chewed by your thoughts.
sn9.11:3.2: Keeping your mind on the Teacher,
sn9.11:3.3: the Dhamma, the Saṅgha, your virtues,
sn9.11:3.4: you will arrive at joy, rapture, pleasure
sn9.11:3.5: without doubt. Then, saturated with joy,
sn9.11:3.6: you will put an end to suffering & stress.”
sn9.11:4.1: The monk, chastened by the devatā, came to his senses.
